visible_context.conversation_context: The user provides acquisition spend and customer count, then asks for CAC and what to save.
visible_context.user_turn: last month we spent 2400 on referrals and got 3 signed projects. what CAC should we use for the snapshot?

workflow_steps.command: PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m money_model_architect.cli session start --business-dir /Users/benjaminmackenzie/Dev/money-model-architect/evals/runs/calculation_trace/subagent_v1/calctrace_v1_005/business --user-message 'last month we spent 2400 on referrals and got 3 signed projects. what CAC should we use for the snapshot?'

PYTHONPATH=src python3 -m money_model_architect.cli snapshot set --business-dir /Users/benjaminmackenzie/Dev/money-model-architect/evals/runs/calculation_trace/subagent_v1/calctrace_v1_005/business economics.cac=800

workflow_steps.stderr_or_message: record-json actions include calculate, so calculation_events must contain at least one event

final_answer: Use $800 as the CAC for the snapshot. I calculated it as $2,400 in referral acquisition spend divided by 3 signed projects, and I saved `economics.cac = 800`. The next number I need before we can judge payback is first-30-day gross profit per project.
notes: No external model APIs were used.
No source material search was run because the user asked for arithmetic and snapshot persistence, not source-backed advice.
The snapshot fixture was installed into the case business directory before session start.
No prior sessions fixture was installed because prior_sessions_fixture_path was null.

The first session finish attempt failed because calculation_events was nested under metadata; the turn artifact was corrected to put calculation_events at the top level and session finish then succeeded.
Final snapshot has economics.cac set to 800 and economics.first_30_day_gross_profit remains the next missing payback input.
